example:
Thieves broke in last night.
She broke in to add a point.
Someone broke in through a window.

Stress can bring on a headache.
The cold brought on her cough.
What brought this on?

The factory closed down.
They closed down the shop.
Many firms closed down.

The truth came out.
Her book comes out in May.
The stain won't come out.

We dressed up for the party.
Kids love to dress up.
You don't need to dress up.

Drop by anytime.
She dropped by for tea.
I'll drop by later.

meaning: have a meal in a restaurant
example:
Let's eat out tonight.
We eat out on Fridays.
They rarely eat out.

Fill out this form.
She filled out the application.
Please fill out both sides.

meaning: leave a place; or become known
example:
Get out of the building!
Let's get out for a walk.
The news got out fast.

The traffic held us up.
Sorry to hold you up.
Robbers held up the bank.

He passed out from the heat.
She nearly passed out.
I almost passed out.

I'll pay you back on Friday.
She paid back the loan.
He never paid me back.

meaning: choose; or recognise from a group
example:
Pick out a dress.
She picked out the right one.
Can you pick him out?

He played down the risk.
They played down the news.
Don't play down your success.

meaning: move to the side of the road and stop
example:
The police told him to pull over.
She pulled over to rest.
Pull over here.

The tickets sold out fast.
We've sold out of bread.
The show sold out.

meaning: rise to your feet; or fail to meet someone
example:
Please stand up.
He stood up to speak.
She stood me up.

A teacher stepped in.
The boss stepped in to help.
Someone had to step in.

Take back the faulty phone.
I take back what I said.
She took back the gift.

I wake up at six.
Wake up, it's late!
She woke up early.
